proposed_hypotheses.text: In this prostate-cancer cohort, higher values of feature_078 (continuous, range 30-90) are associated with longer pfs_months (positive linear association).
Higher values of feature_057 (3-level ordinal, 0/1/2) are associated with shorter pfs_months (negative linear association).
Higher values of feature_013 (continuous, right-skewed, range 0.12-3622) are associated with shorter pfs_months (negative association).
Higher values of feature_009 (continuous, range 1.5-5.5) are associated with longer pfs_months (positive association).

analyses.code: from scipy import stats; stats.pearsonr(df['feature_078'], df['pfs_months'])
analyses.result_summary: Univariate Pearson correlation between feature_078 and pfs_months is r=+0.856, p<1e-300. Decile means rise monotonically from 0.85 months in the lowest decile (29.999-52.3) to 6.86 months in the top decile (77.8-90.0). feature_078 alone explains R^2=0.73 of variance in PFS.
feature_057 is an ordinal 3-level variable (0=17592, 1=24971, 2=7437). Mean PFS is 4.68/3.49/2.38 months at levels 0/1/2 respectively, a monotone decline. Univariate Pearson r=-0.389, p<1e-300.

Raw Pearson r=-0.153 (p~3.5e-260). The variable is heavily right-skewed (median 15.9, 99th pct 417.5, max 3622), so log10 transform is appropriate. Pearson r(log10 feature_013, pfs_months) = -0.231 (p<1e-300). Mean PFS by feature_013 quartile: 4.31/3.93/3.62/3.11 months across Q1-Q4.

Pearson r between feature_009 (mean 3.80, std 0.50) and pfs_months is +0.123 (p~1.5e-166). Modest but highly significant positive association — values consistent with a constitutional/metabolic blood marker (e.g., albumin-shaped distribution).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among the 77 binary 0/1 features in the dataset, feature_051 (prevalence 0.55) has the strongest individual association with pfs_months (negative direction: feature_051=1 patients have shorter PFS than feature_051=0 patients).
feature_067, an ordinal variable taking integer values 6-10 (consistent with a Gleason-score scale), is negatively associated with pfs_months (higher values predict shorter PFS).
Higher values of feature_006 (continuous, range 0-24.6, ~28% zeros) are associated with shorter pfs_months.

analyses.code: for c in binary_cols: stats.ttest_ind(y[df[c]==1], y[df[c]==0])
analyses.result_summary: Across all 77 binary features, feature_051 has the largest signed mean difference (-0.521 months when feature_051=1 vs 0; Welch t-test p~1.6e-181). Mean PFS: 4.03 months when 0 (n=22519) vs 3.51 months when 1 (n=27481). Direction confirms hypothesis.

Despite the 6-10 range that resembles Gleason score, feature_067 has essentially no association with PFS. Mean PFS across grades 6/7/8/9/10 is 3.713/3.764/3.738/3.738/3.703 months (a span of ~0.06 months). Pearson r is approximately 0.000, p>0.5. Hypothesis refuted.

Pearson r=-0.143, p~1e-225. Mean PFS by feature_006 tertile: 3.97 months when feature_006<=3.0 (n=25,293) vs 3.70 (3-6.4) vs 3.33 (6.4-24.6). A binary 'feature_006>0' indicator has a univariate effect of -0.394 months (p~2e-84), so both presence and magnitude matter.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_078, feature_057, log10(feature_013), feature_006, feature_009, and feature_092 are each independent predictors of pfs_months in a multivariable linear regression (each retains a significant signed coefficient when entered jointly), with a combined R^2 above 0.94.

analyses.code: OLS(pfs_months ~ feature_078 + feature_057 + log10(feature_013) + feature_006 + feature_009 + feature_092)
analyses.result_summary: All six predictors are highly significant (each p<1e-100). Coefficients: feature_078=+0.174 mo/unit, feature_057=-1.151 mo/level, log10(feature_013)=-0.804, feature_006=-0.077 mo/unit, feature_009=+0.477 mo/unit, feature_092=-0.0005 mo/unit. Adjusted R^2 = 0.946. Direction of every coefficient matches the univariate sign.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The univariate negative effect of feature_051 on pfs_months is confounded by feature_013 (PSA-like): feature_051=1 patients have markedly higher feature_013 levels, and feature_051's effect should attenuate to zero once log10(feature_013) is included in the model.

analyses.result_summary: feature_051 is strongly tied to feature_013: median feature_013 is 7.4 vs 32.9 (4.5x higher) for feature_051=0 vs 1; t-test on log10(feature_013) by feature_051 status t=150, p<<1e-300. In the full multivariable model that includes log10(feature_013), feature_051's coefficient collapses to -0.001 (p=0.747). The univariate -0.52 month effect is fully explained by the PSA-like marker.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Mean pfs_months differs across the 5 levels of feature_018 (race category: white, black, hispanic, asian, other) in this cohort.

After adjusting for the strongest clinical predictors (feature_078, feature_057, log10(feature_013), feature_006, feature_009, feature_092), pfs_months still differs significantly across racial categories (feature_018) — i.e., a residual disparity remains.

analyses.result_summary: Mean PFS by race: asian 3.797 (n=3019), black 3.741 (n=6064), hispanic 3.737 (n=7562), other 3.749 (n=977), white 3.741 (n=32378). One-way ANOVA F=0.56, p=0.689 — no significant difference. Hypothesis refuted.

After adjusting for the six clinical predictors, no race indicator approaches significance: black coef=+0.002 (p=0.78), hispanic +0.001 (p=0.88), other +0.001 (p=0.96), white +0.003 (p=0.66) — all relative to asian reference. Maximum adjusted between-race difference is ~0.003 months (= 0.1 day). Hypothesis refuted; outcomes are race-blind in this cohort.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Mean pfs_months differs across feature_045 insurance categories (medicare, private, medicaid, uninsured).
After adjusting for the strongest clinical predictors, pfs_months differs significantly across feature_045 insurance categories (a residual socioeconomic disparity).

analyses.result_summary: Mean PFS by insurance: medicaid 3.741 (n=7087), medicare 3.754 (n=21057), private 3.736 (n=19862), uninsured 3.726 (n=1994). ANOVA F=0.34, p=0.794 — no significant difference. Hypothesis refuted.

After clinical adjustment, all insurance dummies are non-significant: medicare coef=+0.0004 (p=0.93), private 0.000 (p=0.99), uninsured 0.000 (p=0.997). Effect sizes are essentially zero. Hypothesis refuted.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with feature_109=1 (prevalence 10%) have longer pfs_months than feature_109=0 patients, and the effect persists after multivariable adjustment.
Patients with feature_039=1 (prevalence 10%) have longer pfs_months than feature_039=0 patients, after multivariable adjustment.
Patients with feature_043=1 (prevalence 20%) have shorter pfs_months than feature_043=0 patients after adjustment.

analyses.result_summary: Adjusted coefficient = +0.151 months (95% CI ~0.140-0.161, p~9e-182). Direction matches hypothesis: feature_109=1 confers ~5 extra days of PFS in the average patient.
Adjusted coefficient = +0.149 months (p~3e-179). Direction matches hypothesis. feature_039 and feature_109 have very similar prevalences (~10%) and very similar adjusted coefficients (~0.15 mo).
Adjusted coefficient = -0.017 months (p~3e-5). Direction matches hypothesis but magnitude is small (~half a day).

proposed_hypotheses.text: There is a feature_078 × feature_057 multiplicative interaction on pfs_months: the positive feature_078 effect is attenuated at higher feature_057 levels (the slope of pfs_months on feature_078 is smaller as feature_057 increases).
feature_051's negative effect on pfs_months is modified by feature_057 (interaction term ≠ 0).

analyses.result_summary: Interaction coefficient = -0.0098 mo/unit/level, p~7.9e-100 — highly significant negative interaction. Per-unit feature_078 slope is reduced by ~0.01 month per increment of feature_057. R^2 rises only from 0.880 to 0.881; the interaction is statistically robust but adds little explanatory power. Direction matches hypothesis.
Interaction coef = +0.027 mo/unit, p=0.0021. feature_051's negative effect is slightly less severe at higher feature_057 levels. Significant but modest.

proposed_hypotheses.text: log10(feature_013) (PSA-like) has a feature_057-stage-modified effect on pfs_months — the log_PSA-PFS slope flattens as feature_057 increases (an interaction in the positive direction relative to the negative main effect).
feature_009 (albumin-like) has a feature_057-modified effect on pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: Interaction coef = +0.042 (p=1.5e-12). log_PSA's negative slope is attenuated by ~0.04 months per stage level. Significant but small relative to the main log_f013 effect of -0.80.
Interaction coef = -0.005, p=0.834. No evidence that feature_009's positive effect varies with feature_057 stage. Hypothesis refuted.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Treatment-like binary features (feature_109, feature_039, feature_043, feature_027) have effects on pfs_months that vary by race (feature_018) — i.e., there is a treatment × race interaction implying differential benefit by race.

analyses.code: joint F-test for {treatment × race_k} interactions in OLS
analyses.result_summary: Joint F-tests for treatment × race interactions: feature_109×race F=0.66 p=0.62; feature_039×race F=1.47 p=0.21; feature_043×race F=0.45 p=0.77; feature_027×race F=0.13 p=0.97. Stratified estimates within each race give very similar coefficients (e.g., feature_051 effect is -0.48 to -0.57 across races). No evidence of treatment-effect heterogeneity by race. Hypothesis refuted.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Treatment-like binary features (feature_109, feature_039, feature_043, feature_027) have effects on pfs_months that vary by feature_045 insurance category — patients with one insurance type benefit more than another.

analyses.result_summary: Joint F-tests: feature_109×insurance F=2.74 p=0.041; feature_039×insurance F=4.19 p=0.0057; feature_043×insurance F=0.26 p=0.85; feature_027×insurance F=0.20 p=0.90. Stratified treatment effects: feature_039 coefficients are 0.16/0.13/0.19/0.16 across medicare/private/uninsured/medicaid (range 0.06 mo, ~2 days); feature_109 effects are 0.15/0.14/0.15/0.18. Statistically significant heterogeneity for feature_039 and feature_109 but the absolute effect-size spread across groups is < 1 week. Hypothesis weakly supported but clinically negligible.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The five 0-4 ordinal features (feature_016, feature_071, feature_096, feature_033, feature_064) and the 0-10 ordinal feature_026 each have a non-zero linear association with pfs_months in univariate regression.
At least one of these ordinal features modifies the effect of feature_051 or feature_109 (interaction p<0.05).

analyses.result_summary: Pearson r and p (all univariate): feature_016 r=-0.006 p=0.21; feature_071 r=+0.002 p=0.66; feature_096 r=-0.001 p=0.81; feature_033 r=+0.001 p=0.79; feature_064 r=-0.001 p=0.91; feature_026 r=-0.001 p=0.80. None comes close to significance. Hypothesis refuted; these ordinal features are uninformative for PFS in this cohort.

analyses.code: OLS with interaction terms ordinal × feature_051, ordinal × feature_109
analyses.result_summary: Interaction tests: ordinal × feature_051 p-values were 0.32/0.81/0.35/0.33/0.29/0.58 across the six ordinal features; ordinal × feature_109 p-values were 0.19/0.19/0.62/0.19/0.12/0.67. None reaches p<0.05. Hypothesis refuted.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_078's effect on pfs_months is non-linear: a quadratic term (feature_078^2) is significantly different from zero with positive sign (curvature implies the per-unit effect grows at higher values).

analyses.result_summary: Quadratic coefficient = +0.00078, p~1.9e-112 (significant). However, R^2 only rises from 0.7319 (linear) to 0.7346 (quadratic). The non-linearity is statistically detectable in 50,000 patients but adds <0.3% explanatory power. Direction matches hypothesis (positive curvature).

proposed_hypotheses.text: There is a three-way interaction feature_078 × feature_057 × feature_051 on pfs_months — the modifying effect of feature_057 on the feature_078 slope itself depends on feature_051 status.

analyses.result_summary: Three-way interaction term coefficient = -0.0053 (p~6e-10). Statistically significant at very large n; effect is small in magnitude but the negative sign means the feature_078 × feature_057 attenuation is larger when feature_051=1.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_120 (continuous, range 20-508) is associated with pfs_months (non-zero linear effect).
feature_092 (continuous, range 48-830) is independently negatively associated with pfs_months in the multivariable model, even though the per-unit effect is tiny.

analyses.result_summary: Univariate r=+0.001 (p=0.83). Adjusted coef in multivariable model = -3e-5 (p=0.18). No association in either form. Hypothesis refuted.

analyses.code: OLS final model coefficient for feature_092
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted coefficient = -0.00052 mo/unit, p<1e-100 (t~-27). Across the observed range (48-830), the modeled difference is ~ -0.4 months from low to high. Significant but small per-unit; direction confirms hypothesis.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among the binary indicator 'feature_006 > 0', presence (=1) is associated with shorter pfs_months relative to absence (=0).

analyses.result_summary: 27.5% of patients have feature_006=0. Univariate effect of being feature_006>0: -0.394 months (p~2e-84). Linear feature_006 also has a strong adjusted slope of -0.077 mo/unit, so the variable carries information both as a presence indicator and as a magnitude.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Stratifying by feature_013 (PSA-like) quartile, mean pfs_months declines monotonically from Q1 to Q4.

analyses.result_summary: Mean pfs_months across feature_013 quartiles Q1/Q2/Q3/Q4 (cutoffs 6.46/15.86/41.28): 4.31/3.93/3.62/3.11 — strictly decreasing as PSA-like values increase. Difference Q1-Q4 = 1.20 months. Hypothesis supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among low-prevalence (≤5%) binary features, at least one carries an adjusted effect on pfs_months that is significant at p<0.05 in a multivariable model controlling for feature_078, feature_057, log_f013, feature_006, feature_009, feature_092.

analyses.code: for each binary c with prev<=0.05: OLS(pfs ~ base + c)
analyses.result_summary: Among low-prevalence binary features, feature_121 (prev=0.051) has adjusted coef = +0.021 (p=0.004) and feature_058 (prev=0.019) has adjusted coef = +0.024 (p=0.041). Other rare features (feature_022 0.05, feature_073 0.048, feature_001 0.029) are not significant after adjustment. Hypothesis supported, but absolute effect sizes are small (<1 day).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within insurance strata (feature_045), residual race-effects on pfs_months (after adjusting for clinical predictors) emerge that were masked in the marginal analysis.

analyses.code: OLS(pfs ~ base + race_dummies) within each insurance class
analyses.result_summary: Within each insurance stratum, all race dummies have p>0.2 with adjusted coefficients in the range -0.04 to +0.01 months. No evidence of insurance-conditional racial disparities. Largest single estimate is race_other in uninsured (n=23) at -0.041 mo (p=0.56). Hypothesis refuted.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_027 (binary, prevalence 0.60) is independently associated with longer pfs_months in the multivariable model (positive direction).
feature_121 (binary, prevalence 0.05) is independently associated with longer pfs_months in the multivariable model.

analyses.result_summary: Adjusted coefficient = +0.025 mo (p~2e-15, t=7.9). Direction matches hypothesis but magnitude (~0.8 day) is small.
Adjusted coefficient = +0.019 mo (p=0.007). Direction matches hypothesis; magnitude ~0.6 day.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_109 and feature_039 (each prev ~0.10, each adjusted +0.15 mo) interact super-additively: patients with both =1 have an extra benefit beyond the sum of the two main effects.

analyses.result_summary: Interaction coef = -0.005 mo, p=0.86. No evidence of synergy or antagonism between feature_109 and feature_039. Effects appear independent and approximately additive. Hypothesis refuted.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_078 and feature_009 interact on pfs_months: the per-unit effect of feature_009 (positive) is amplified at higher feature_078 (positive interaction term).

analyses.result_summary: Interaction coefficient is small (~0.002, p~0.4 in models including base predictors). No robust evidence of feature_078 × feature_009 interaction. Hypothesis refuted.

proposed_hypotheses.text: A model with feature_078, feature_057, log10(feature_013), feature_006, feature_009, feature_092, feature_109, feature_039, feature_043, feature_027, feature_121 explains >97% of variance in pfs_months and every coefficient retains the same signed direction it had in the simpler model.

analyses.result_summary: R^2 = 0.9704 (Adj R^2 = 0.9703). All 11 coefficients have the same signs as in the simpler 6-predictor model and remain highly significant: feature_078 +0.174 (p<1e-300), feature_057 -1.151, log_f013 -0.805, feature_006 -0.077, feature_009 +0.477, feature_092 -0.0005, feature_109 +0.151, feature_039 +0.148, feature_043 -0.017, feature_027 +0.025, feature_121 +0.019. Hypothesis supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_078 is approximately uncorrelated with feature_057, feature_067, and the 0-4 ordinals (feature_016/071/096/033/064) and feature_026 (each |Pearson r|<0.02), consistent with feature_078 acting as an independent constitutional predictor.

analyses.code: for c in ordinals: stats.pearsonr(df['feature_078'], df[c])
analyses.result_summary: All pairwise correlations of feature_078 with the ordinal variables: feature_057 r=-0.004 p=0.32; feature_067 r=+0.001 p=0.87; feature_016 r=-0.008 p=0.06; feature_071 r=+0.003 p=0.50; feature_096 r=0.000 p=0.98; feature_033 r=0.000 p=0.94; feature_064 r=-0.004 p=0.41; feature_026 r=+0.001 p=0.86. All |r|<0.01. feature_078 is essentially orthogonal to the ordinals — its dominant effect on PFS is not confounded by them. Hypothesis supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Summary check: in this 50,000-patient prostate cohort there is no clinically meaningful disparity in pfs_months by race (feature_018) or by insurance (feature_045), either marginally or after adjustment for clinical predictors, and no race-modified treatment effect for the four candidate treatment-like binary features (feature_109, feature_039, feature_043, feature_027).

analyses.result_summary: Marginal race ANOVA p=0.69; marginal insurance ANOVA p=0.79; adjusted race coefficients all |effect|<0.005 (all p>0.6); adjusted insurance coefficients all |effect|<0.001 (all p>0.9); treatment×race joint F-tests p>0.2 for all four candidate treatments; treatment×insurance joint F-test reached p<0.05 for feature_109 and feature_039 but the spread of stratified treatment-effect estimates was <1 week across all four insurance groups (medicare/private/medicaid/uninsured). Conclusion: outcomes in this cohort are essentially race- and insurance-blind, both at the main-effect level and at the level of treatment-effect heterogeneity. Hypothesis supported.
